summary: Decompiled multiple item-state functions for Unown, Butterfree, Tingle, and Goldeen item modules, replacing placeholder comments and UNK prototypes with typed implementations. The PR also split padding in several item variable and attribute structs to expose fields required by the new code. The submitted verification reported all functions 100% matching in objdiff and main.dol SHA1 OK.
what_changed: Implemented `it_802CE710` and `itUnknown_UnkMotion0_Anim` in `itunknown.c` for Unown setup and out-of-bounds animation handling.
Changed `it_802CE8D0` in `itunknown.h` from `UNK_RET it_802CE8D0(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void it_802CE8D0(Item_GObj*)`.
Implemented `itPatapata_UnkMotion4_Anim` in `itpatapata.c`, including a countdown/toggle field and visibility flag changes on dynamic bone table bones 18 and 11.
Implemented `it_802E15B0` in `itpatapata.c`, spawning a related Nokonoko item via `it_802DD7F0`, transferring knockback, angle, damage direction, and item-var state, then calling `it_802DCE00`.
Changed `it_802E15B0` in `itpatapata.h` from an unknown signature to `void it_802E15B0(Item_GObj*)`.
Implemented `itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll` in `itoldottosea.c`, using collision helpers `it_8026D8A4`, `it_80276308`, `it_802E3098`, `it_802E2BC0`, and returning `it_802E35CC`.
Expanded `itTincle_ItemVars` with concrete float fields at offsets `x30`, `x40`, `x44`, `x48` and an `itECB x68` field.
Expanded `itTincleAttributes` with fields including `s32 x40`, `s8 x54`, and `s8 x55`.
Expanded `itPatapata_ItemVars` with `s32 x4` and `s32 x44`, and `itPatapataAttributes` with `s32 x28` and `s32 x2C`.
Expanded `itUnknownAttributes` with `HSD_Joint* x24[26]`, used by Unown initialization to choose a random joint/model entry.
smart_moves: The PR exposed only the struct fields needed by the matched functions instead of fully over-naming unknown item data, preserving offset evidence while enabling typed C.
Header prototypes were tightened from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete `void` and `Item_GObj*` signatures where the implementations proved the calling convention.

The Tingle ECB adjustment copied `ip->xDD4_itemVar.tincle.x68` into a local `itECB`, conditionally scaled selected dimensions by `0.5f`, then passed the adjusted copy to `it_80275D5C`, matching behavior without mutating the stored original ECB.
For random timing in `it_802EC4D0`, the code handles negative `data->x55 - data->x54` by taking the absolute value before `HSD_Randi(range)`, then offsets by `x54`.
Butterfree animation used explicit `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll` and `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll` on dynamic bone table entries to express the model-part toggle observed in assembly.
The Unown initializer modeled the random letter/joint selection as `attr->x24[HSD_Randi(26)]`, which also justified adding the 26-element `HSD_Joint*` array to `itUnknownAttributes`.
decomp_lessons: When item functions touch `xDD4_itemVar` or special attributes, splitting padding at exact offsets can be the key to converting placeholder code into readable matching C.
Keep unknown field names offset-based, such as `x20`, `x44`, or `x68`, when semantics are not proven; this PR improved types and layout without inventing gameplay names.
Replacing `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` should be done when the decompiled function body proves the return type and argument list.

Item modules often require includes from nearby systems once behavior is decompiled: this slice added dependencies such as `gr/stage.h`, `lb/lb_00B0.h`, `cm/camera.h`, `it/items/itnokonoko.h`, `baselib/jobj.h`, and `baselib/random.h`.

Out-of-bounds item behavior can be expressed through stage blast zone helpers rather than hardcoded bounds; `itUnknown_UnkMotion0_Anim` checks all four `Stage_GetBlastZone*Offset` values before calling its cleanup/transition helper.

For matching, preserve apparently non-semantic stack padding when needed; this PR retained `PAD_STACK(8)` in functions such as `itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll`, `itTincle_UnkMotion7_Anim`, and `it_802EC4D0`.
naming_conventions: Existing item-state names with unknown motion labels were preserved, e.g. `itUnknown_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `itPatapata_UnkMotion4_Anim`, and `itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll`.
Unidentified functions retained address-derived names such as `it_802CE710`, `it_802E15B0`, `it_802EBE5C`, `it_802EC4D0`, and `it_802EC9E8`.
Struct fields with uncertain semantics were named by offset, such as `itTincle_ItemVars.x68`, `itPatapata_ItemVars.x44`, `itPatapataAttributes.x2C`, and `itUnknownAttributes.x24`.
Typed struct names followed existing item-specific conventions: `itTincle_ItemVars`, `itTincleAttributes`, `itPatapata_ItemVars`, `itPatapataAttributes`, and `itUnknownAttributes`.
Header prototypes use concrete item object types where known, e.g. `void it_802E15B0(Item_GObj*)` and `void it_802CE8D0(Item_GObj*)`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in several functions to preserve stack layout for matching.
Used exact float constants and assignments such as zeroing all three velocity components and scaling ECB fields by `0.5f`.
Structured `itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Coll` with nested conditionals around `it_8026D8A4`, `ip->x40_vel.x != 0.0f`, and `it_80276308` to match control flow.
Used local temporaries such as `s32 toggle`, `s32 range`, `Vec3 pos`, `Item_GObj* new_gobj`, and `Item* new_ip` where needed for matching and readability.
Included baselib and system headers only after function bodies required concrete calls or types such as `HSD_Randi`, `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll`, `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll`, `HSD_JObj`, and `Camera_80030E44`.
Modeled random branch behavior directly with `HSD_Randi(26)` for Unown joint selection and `HSD_Randi(2)` for direction sign.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body reported verification evidence: all functions at 100% match via objdiff and `main.dol: OK` with SHA1 verified.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or further type `itTincle_ItemVars` fields `x20`, `x30`, `x40`, `x44`, `x48`, and `x68`.
Look for later PRs that identify the semantics of `itPatapata_ItemVars.x4`, `x28`, `x40`, and `x44` or the spawned Nokonoko relationship in `it_802E15B0`.
Look for later PRs that document `itUnknownAttributes.x24[26]` as Unown model or joint data.
Search for related decomp work on `it_802EC870`, `it_802E11E0`, `itPatapata_UnkMotion4_Phys`, and remaining placeholder functions near these modules.
